product.name: BooksyAI
product.tagline: Your shelf. Your taste. Your AI.
product.summary: BooksyAI is a book discovery web app with a warm, editorial feel. It combines a curated catalog (grounded in a local knowledge base), personalized onboarding, shelves you can organize, a community feed for reviews and follows, and an AI chat that recommends real titles from the catalog instead of inventing books.

designAndTone.visual: Light, warm palette (cream, soft browns, gentle gradients), serif-style headings for a literary feel, and subtle motion on key sections.
designAndTone.voice: Friendly, concise, and honest about what the product can do. Recommendations explain why a title fits; the assistant does not fake metadata.

mainUserJourneys.name: Home
mainUserJourneys.whatYouSee: Hero, featured books, mood strips, and calls-to-action toward browsing, onboarding, and signing in.

mainUserJourneys.name: Browse catalog
mainUserJourneys.whatYouSee: Filter and paginate the curated catalog by genre, mood, language, and more. Every listed book exists in the app’s knowledge base.

mainUserJourneys.name: Book detail
mainUserJourneys.whatYouSee: Synopsis, ratings, genres, similar titles, optional reviews UI, and a shelf control to mark Want to read / Reading / Read when signed in.

mainUserJourneys.name: Taste profile (onboarding)
mainUserJourneys.whatYouSee: A short wizard to capture reading tastes and goals so recommendations and home content feel more relevant.

mainUserJourneys.name: Dashboard (your shelf)
mainUserJourneys.whatYouSee: Signed-in home: your Want · Reading · Done lists, mood preferences, recommendations, and community pulse — the main place your shelf lives.

mainUserJourneys.name: Community
mainUserJourneys.whatYouSee: Community feed of reviews, follow readers, and shared reading activity.

mainUserJourneys.name: About BooksyAI
mainUserJourneys.whatYouSee: Mission, values, and how BooksyAI thinks about taste, transparency, and community.

mainUserJourneys.name: Notifications
mainUserJourneys.whatYouSee: In-app notices about recommendations, shelf activity, and community signals (where enabled).

mainUserJourneys.name: Reading analytics
mainUserJourneys.whatYouSee: Charts and stats about reading patterns for signed-in users.

mainUserJourneys.name: Public profiles
mainUserJourneys.whatYouSee: A reader-facing profile URL when profiles are available.

mainUserJourneys.name: Legal
mainUserJourneys.whatYouSee: Privacy policy and terms of use.

mainUserJourneys.name: Auth
mainUserJourneys.whatYouSee: Sign-in and account creation use the global dialog (also opened via legacy /auth/login and /auth/signup, which redirect home with query params). The floating chat and shelf saving generally expect a signed-in session for anything that writes to your account.

booksyAiChat.whatItIs: A floating assistant on the site that helps you pick books using the same curated catalog as the rest of BooksyAI.
booksyAiChat.howItWorks: Your recent messages are used to search the knowledge base for matching titles. The assistant should recommend books that appear in the catalog snippet provided to it and can point you to paths like /book/<slug>.
booksyAiChat.limitations: It will not invent books, authors, or ratings. If nothing in the catalog fits, it should say so and still offer general reading guidance.
booksyAiChat.starters: Example prompts are suggested when the chat is empty (e.g. quick mood-based or comp-title asks).

accountAndPrivacy.signIn: Signing in unlocks saving shelf status, dashboard, notifications, and other account-backed features.
accountAndPrivacy.catalogIntegrity: Titles come from BooksyAI’s curated knowledge base; arbitrary user-typed books are not added to the shared catalog from the shelf API.
accountAndPrivacy.dataPhilosophy: Share only what you are comfortable making part of your reader profile; see /legal/privacy for details.

valuesFromAbout: Recommendations are grounded in a real catalog — traceable picks, not hallucinated bestsellers.
Taste matters more than raw engagement metrics.
Community features connect readers with overlapping taste.
The UI aims to stay focused: minimal clutter, no dark patterns in the product narrative.
